todaysEvents.summary: Trump says Iran MOU 'largely negotiated'; 60-day framework: Hormuz open, Iran oil sales, Phase 2 nuclear talks; Brent -6% past week. (May 23/26)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Trump declared deal 'largely negotiated' on May 23 (CNBC, CNN, NPR); 60-day Phase 1 framework - Hormuz reopens, no tolls, Iran clears mines, Iran oil sales resume, US lifts blockade + issues sanctions waivers; Phase 2 = nuclear talks within 30-60 days. Brent crashed from $104.52 (D81 May 22) to $98.21 (D85 May 26; TradingEconomics +0.99% day-on-day rebound); WTI $92.58 (TradingEconomics +1.74%); oil -6% week on Hormuz deal optimism

todaysEvents.summary: Deal disputes: nuclear sequencing unresolved; Iran's Fars: Hormuz 'under Iran management'; Khamenei uranium directive still standing. (May 24)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Deal framework disputed on key terms (CNN May 24, Axios May 24); Iran's Fars news agency: Strait of Hormuz would remain 'under Iran management' - contradicts US framing; nuclear talks deferred to Phase 2 while Khamenei uranium directive (May 22) still requires uranium to remain in Iran; US wants nuclear commitment in Phase 1. Both sides claim 'deal largely done' while disputing what is in it. Tail (23%) reflects collapse risk if sequencing impasse hardens before signature

Down: Confirmed prices (TradingEconomics May 26): Brent $98.21 (+0.99%); WTI $92.58 (+1.74%); Gold $4,530.61 (-0.85%); TTF €46.71 (+2.47% rebound from -4.2% D84 low). JKM $18.81 est. (Platts May 22; Canada LNG Group May 25 weekly not yet published). iTraxx Asia IG ~178bp (-6bp est.); ASEAN HY ~550bp (-13bp est.). Tail 23% (down from 38% on deal 'largely negotiated' signal; Base 30%; 3pp base-case shift vs D81 15%)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump declares deal 'largely negotiated' May 23 (CNBC, CNN, NPR); 60-day Phase 1 framework: Hormuz reopens, Iran clears mines, Iran oil sales resume, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers; Phase 2 nuclear talks within 30-60 days. If signature confirmed: Brent retreats toward $80-90/bbl; P&I restoration enables commercial traffic; Ras Laffan restart timeline set; iTraxx tightens toward 155-160bp. Widened from D81 15% - deal has real momentum but nuclear sequencing + Khamenei uranium directive still unresolved; Fars disputes Strait control framing. Not confirmed until signed.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Deal signed but implementation crawls: Phase 1 Hormuz partial reopening while nuclear sequencing dispute delays Phase 2; Khamenei uranium directive creates Phase 2 red line; mine-clearing ~6 months delays full commercial recovery; P&I clubs restore conditionally; oil stabilises $90-110/bbl. TTF €42-50/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 165-200bp. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal. Dominant scenario (47%) - partial normalisation, not resolution.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapses before signature: nuclear sequencing impasse hardens (Khamenei uranium directive vs US nuclear-first demand); Iran's Fars Strait control dispute triggers US walkout; Trump resumes strikes; IRGC re-escalates beyond current blockade. Brent spikes $125-165/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting; Malaysia July-August supply crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (23%) - reduced from D81 38% on 'largely negotiated' signal; Khamenei directive + Strait sovereignty dispute are the primary collapse triggers.
